title: Design Ontology
description: A machine-traversable knowledge graph encoding the reasoning behind design decisions — from user intent through psychology, culture, and audience to concrete visual properties.

anti_patterns.title: Anti-Patterns
anti_patterns.description: Design decisions explicitly prohibited by this ontology. Keys are anti-pattern IDs (ANTI-xxx format).

meta.title: Ontology Metadata
meta.description: Metadata describing the ontology: what product it serves, when it was generated, and its version.

product.title: Product Name
product.description: The name of the product this ontology describes.

version.title: Ontology Version
version.description: Semantic version of this ontology file (e.g., "1.0.0").

generated.title: Generation Date
generated.description: ISO 8601 date string indicating when this ontology was generated or last updated.

purpose.title: Ontology Purpose
purpose.description: A brief statement of what this ontology encodes and why it exists for this product.

schema_version.title: Schema Version
schema_version.description: The version of the design-ontology schema this file conforms to. Used for future migration support.

node_id.title: Node ID
node_id.description: A unique identifier for a node. Format: starts with an uppercase letter, followed by 1-4 uppercase letters or digits, then a hyphen and 3 digits (e.g., INT-001, PSY-012, VIS-003, A11Y-001).

base_node.title: Base Node
base_node.description: The base structure shared by all ontology nodes. Every node must have an ID, label, description, and a list of outgoing connections.

id.title: Node ID
id.description: The unique identifier for this node. Must match the key under which this node is stored in its domain group.

label.title: Node Label
label.description: A human-readable name for this node (e.g., "Risk Assessment", "Visual Hierarchy").

description.title: Node Description
description.description: A detailed explanation of what this node represents and why it exists in the ontology.

short.title: Short Description
short.description: A concise label for use in visualizations and compact displays.

connects_to.title: Outgoing Connections
connects_to.description: Array of node IDs that this node connects to. Represents directional relationships in the ontology graph. Referential integrity: every ID listed here must exist as a node key somewhere in the nodes section. This constraint is enforced programmatically, not by this schema.

principle.title: Design Principle
principle.description: The design principle this node embodies (e.g., "Progressive disclosure reduces cognitive load").

implication.title: Design Implication
implication.description: What this node implies for the design — the actionable consequence of the principle.

anti_pattern.title: Anti-Pattern Reference
anti_pattern.description: A brief description of the design anti-pattern associated with this node — what NOT to do.

specification.title: Specification
specification.description: A concrete specification or value for this node (e.g., "font-size: 16px", "border-radius: 8px").

property_type.title: Property Type
property_type.description: The type of visual property this node represents (e.g., "color", "typography", "spacing", "layout").

derived_from.title: Derived From
derived_from.description: Array of node IDs that this node's reasoning is derived from. Referential integrity: every ID must exist as a node key. Enforced programmatically.

reasoning.title: Reasoning
reasoning.description: The reasoning chain that led to this node's inclusion — why this design decision was made.

rule.title: Rule
rule.description: A concrete, implementable rule derived from this node (e.g., "Always show confidence intervals alongside risk scores").

phase.title: Phase
phase.description: The user journey phase this node applies to (e.g., "onboarding", "active-use", "error-recovery").

state.title: State
state.description: The UI or user state this node is relevant to (e.g., "loading", "empty", "error", "success").

ui_response.title: UI Response
ui_response.description: How the UI should respond when this node's conditions are met.

role.title: Role
role.description: The user role or persona this node is most relevant to.

expertise.title: Expertise Level
expertise.description: The expertise level assumed for this node (e.g., "novice", "intermediate", "expert").

constraints.title: Constraints
constraints.description: Technical or design constraints that apply to this node.

linguistic_examples.title: Linguistic Examples
linguistic_examples.description: Example phrases, labels, or microcopy that embody this node's intent or tone.

domain_group.title: Domain Group
domain_group.description: A collection of nodes within a single domain. Keys are node IDs, values are node objects.

nodes.title: Ontology Nodes
nodes.description: All nodes in the ontology, organized by domain group. The seven domains represent layers of design reasoning: from user intents through psychological and cultural factors to concrete visual properties and accessibility requirements.

intents.title: Intent Nodes
intents.description: User intents — what users are trying to accomplish. These are the starting points for reasoning chains. Nodes use INT-xxx IDs.

psychology.title: Psychology Nodes
psychology.description: Cognitive and psychological principles that influence design decisions (e.g., cognitive load, attention patterns, trust formation). Nodes use PSY-xxx IDs.

culture.title: Culture Nodes
culture.description: Cultural context and conventions that shape how design is perceived by the target audience (e.g., color associations, reading patterns, formality expectations). Nodes use CUL-xxx IDs.

emotions.title: Emotion Nodes
emotions.description: Emotional states and responses the design should evoke, manage, or support (e.g., trust, anxiety reduction, confidence). Nodes use EMO-xxx IDs.

audience.title: Audience Nodes
audience.description: Target audience segments and their characteristics, expertise levels, and needs. Nodes use AUD-xxx IDs.

visual_properties.title: Visual Property Nodes
visual_properties.description: Concrete visual specifications derived from the reasoning chains — colors, typography, spacing, layout, and other implementable properties. These are typically the endpoints of reasoning chains. Nodes use VIS-xxx IDs.

accessibility.title: Accessibility Nodes
accessibility.description: Accessibility requirements, guidelines, and considerations that constrain and inform visual property decisions. Nodes use A11Y-xxx IDs.

reasoning_chain.title: Reasoning Chain
reasoning_chain.description: A named path through the ontology graph connecting nodes from intent to visual properties. Chains encode the WHY behind design decisions — the reasoning path from what a user wants to accomplish to the concrete visual properties that support it.

id.title: Chain ID
id.description: Unique identifier for this reasoning chain (CHAIN-xxx format).

name.title: Chain Name
name.description: A human-readable name describing the reasoning path (e.g., "Risk Assessment to Visual Hierarchy").

path.title: Chain Path
path.description: Ordered array of node IDs forming the reasoning path. Typically starts with an intent node and ends with visual property nodes, passing through psychology, culture, emotion, and audience nodes. Minimum 3 nodes. Referential integrity: every ID must exist as a node key. Enforced programmatically.

description.title: Chain Description
description.description: A narrative explanation of the reasoning this chain represents — why these nodes connect in this order.

weight.title: Chain Weight
weight.description: Importance weight from 1 (supplementary) to 5 (critical). Higher-weight chains take priority when reasoning conflicts arise.

edges.title: Ontology Edges
edges.description: Edges connecting nodes across the ontology. Currently contains reasoning chains — named, weighted paths through the graph.

reasoning_chains.title: Reasoning Chains
reasoning_chains.description: Array of reasoning chains, each representing a named path from intent to visual properties through intermediate reasoning nodes.

anti_pattern.title: Anti-Pattern
anti_pattern.description: A design decision explicitly prohibited by this ontology. Anti-patterns encode what NOT to do and trace back to the nodes whose principles they violate.

label.title: Anti-Pattern Label
label.description: A short, recognizable name for this anti-pattern (e.g., "Generic Dashboard Layout", "Decorative Animations").

description.title: Anti-Pattern Description
description.description: What this anti-pattern looks like in practice — the design decision being prohibited.

why_prohibited.title: Why Prohibited
why_prohibited.description: The reasoning for why this design approach is prohibited, referencing the ontology's principles and audience needs.

traces_to.title: Traces To
traces_to.description: Array of node IDs whose principles this anti-pattern violates. Referential integrity: every ID must exist as a node key. Enforced programmatically.

generation_instructions.title: Generation Instructions
generation_instructions.description: Optional instructions for AI agents on how to use this ontology during code generation. Includes a recommended process and an example traversal.

process.title: Generation Process
process.description: Ordered list of steps an AI agent should follow when generating UI code using this ontology.

example_traversal.title: Example Traversal
example_traversal.description: A worked example showing how to traverse the ontology from intent to visual properties for a specific component.
